questions.stem:
—I ______ the Great Wall twice. It's amazing.
—Really? When ______ you ______ there last time?
questions.explanation: 第一空用现在完成时have visited，表示"曾经去过两次"，强调对现在的影响或经历；第二空有明确时间状语last time，用一般过去时did...go。B和D第一空错误，C第二空错误。技巧：看到twice/ever/never等用现在完成时；看到last time/yesterday等具体过去时间用一般过去时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ cover is red belongs to Tom. It is about science.
questions.explanation: 先行词是the boy（人），且在定语从句中作主语，用who。which用于指物（排除B）；whose表示"...的"，后需接名词（排除C）；whom用于指人但作宾语（排除D）。技巧：先行词是人且作主语用who，作宾语用who/whom，表示所属用whose；先行词是物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the school library after they are read.
questions.explanation: 主语books和动词return是被动关系（书被归还），含情态动词must的被动语态结构是：must be + 过去分词。A是主动语态；C缺少be；D中return形式错误。技巧：被动语态基本结构be done，情态动词后be用原形。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Do you think ______ we will have a picnic tomorrow?
—I'm not sure. It ______ rain in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: 第一空：根据答语I'm not sure（不确定），可知问句是"是否"，用if或whether，排除A和D；第二空：表示"可能下雨"用might（可能性较小）或may。must表示肯定推测"一定"，与not sure矛盾；can表推测通常用于否定句。因此选B。技巧：不确定用might/may，肯定推测用must，否定推测用can't。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are not ______ with your current grade, you should work harder to get a better one next time.
questions.explanation: be satisfied with是固定搭配，表示"对...感到满意"。主语是人，用-ed形容词satisfied；satisfying令人满意的（修饰物）；satisfaction是名词；satisfy是动词。技巧：-ed形容词修饰人，-ing形容词修饰物。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ news made all of us feel ______. We jumped with joy.
questions.explanation: 第一空修饰news（物），用exciting（令人兴奋的）；第二空修饰us（人），用excited（感到兴奋的）。技巧：-ing形容词表示"令人...的"，修饰物；-ed形容词表示"感到...的"，修饰人。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ our best to reduce plastic waste and protect the environment.
questions.explanation: try one's best是固定短语，意为"尽某人最大努力"。make one's best（无此搭配）；do one's best虽然也有"尽力"之意，但通常说do one's best，而try one's best更常用；give one's best（无此搭配）。根据常用搭配，选A。技巧：try one's best = do one's best，但选项中只有try符合。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Green Life in Our School

Our school started a "Green Campus" project this term. The goal is to make students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

Every classroom now has three different bins. They are for paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (8)______ to remember which bin to use. But after two weeks, everyone got used to it.

The most popular part is the "No Plastic Week". During this week, students cannot use plastic bags or bottles. Instead, we use cloth bags and metal bottles. Some students thought it would be (9)______ to live without plastic. However, they soon found it was easier than expected.

What surprised us most was the "Green Garden" on the rooftop. Students can plant vegetables there. Not only does it provide fresh food, but it also teaches us about nature. Working in the garden has become a favorite activity. Students say it helps them (10)______ from study pressure.

The project has run for three months. According to the data, waste produced by our school has dropped by 30%. This result (11)______ us that small changes can make a big difference. Our headmaster said, "We plan to add solar panels next year. I hope more students will (12)______ in making our school greener."
questions.explanation: 根据下文"But after two weeks, everyone got used to it（但两周后大家习惯了）"的转折关系，可知起初应该是"困难的"。easy（容易的）与but转折矛盾；possible（可能的）和necessary（必要的）不符合语境。技巧：注意but/however等转折词，前后语义相反。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Green Life in Our School

Our school started a "Green Campus" project this term. The goal is to make students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

Every classroom now has three different bins. They are for paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (8)______ to remember which bin to use. But after two weeks, everyone got used to it.

The most popular part is the "No Plastic Week". During this week, students cannot use plastic bags or bottles. Instead, we use cloth bags and metal bottles. Some students thought it would be (9)______ to live without plastic. However, they soon found it was easier than expected.

What surprised us most was the "Green Garden" on the rooftop. Students can plant vegetables there. Not only does it provide fresh food, but it also teaches us about nature. Working in the garden has become a favorite activity. Students say it helps them (10)______ from study pressure.

The project has run for three months. According to the data, waste produced by our school has dropped by 30%. This result (11)______ us that small changes can make a big difference. Our headmaster said, "We plan to add solar panels next year. I hope more students will (12)______ in making our school greener."
questions.explanation: 根据下文"However, they soon found it was easier than expected（然而他们很快发现比预期容易）"，可知起初认为"困难"。however表示转折，后面说easier，前面应该是difficult。技巧：however/ but前后语义相反，利用反义词解题。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Green Life in Our School

Our school started a "Green Campus" project this term. The goal is to make students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

Every classroom now has three different bins. They are for paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (8)______ to remember which bin to use. But after two weeks, everyone got used to it.

The most popular part is the "No Plastic Week". During this week, students cannot use plastic bags or bottles. Instead, we use cloth bags and metal bottles. Some students thought it would be (9)______ to live without plastic. However, they soon found it was easier than expected.

What surprised us most was the "Green Garden" on the rooftop. Students can plant vegetables there. Not only does it provide fresh food, but it also teaches us about nature. Working in the garden has become a favorite activity. Students say it helps them (10)______ from study pressure.

The project has run for three months. According to the data, waste produced by our school has dropped by 30%. This result (11)______ us that small changes can make a big difference. Our headmaster said, "We plan to add solar panels next year. I hope more students will (12)______ in making our school greener."
questions.explanation: help sb. (to) do sth.是固定搭配，意为"帮助某人做某事"，to可以省略。因此用动词原形relax。技巧：help后接不带to或带to的不定式作宾补。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Green Life in Our School

Our school started a "Green Campus" project this term. The goal is to make students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

Every classroom now has three different bins. They are for paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (8)______ to remember which bin to use. But after two weeks, everyone got used to it.

The most popular part is the "No Plastic Week". During this week, students cannot use plastic bags or bottles. Instead, we use cloth bags and metal bottles. Some students thought it would be (9)______ to live without plastic. However, they soon found it was easier than expected.

What surprised us most was the "Green Garden" on the rooftop. Students can plant vegetables there. Not only does it provide fresh food, but it also teaches us about nature. Working in the garden has become a favorite activity. Students say it helps them (10)______ from study pressure.

The project has run for three months. According to the data, waste produced by our school has dropped by 30%. This result (11)______ us that small changes can make a big difference. Our headmaster said, "We plan to add solar panels next year. I hope more students will (12)______ in making our school greener."
questions.explanation: 根据语境"这个结果_____我们小改变能带来大不同"，tells（告诉）符合语境。reminds（提醒）需搭配of/from；warns（警告）语气过重；promises（承诺）主语通常是人。技巧：根据宾语从句内容判断动词，客观事实用tell。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Green Life in Our School

Our school started a "Green Campus" project this term. The goal is to make students understand the importance of protecting the environment.

Every classroom now has three different bins. They are for paper, plastic, and other waste. At first, many students found it (8)______ to remember which bin to use. But after two weeks, everyone got used to it.

The most popular part is the "No Plastic Week". During this week, students cannot use plastic bags or bottles. Instead, we use cloth bags and metal bottles. Some students thought it would be (9)______ to live without plastic. However, they soon found it was easier than expected.

What surprised us most was the "Green Garden" on the rooftop. Students can plant vegetables there. Not only does it provide fresh food, but it also teaches us about nature. Working in the garden has become a favorite activity. Students say it helps them (10)______ from study pressure.

The project has run for three months. According to the data, waste produced by our school has dropped by 30%. This result (11)______ us that small changes can make a big difference. Our headmaster said, "We plan to add solar panels next year. I hope more students will (12)______ in making our school greener."
questions.explanation: join in doing sth.表示"参与做某事"。take part需加in；attend后接会议/学校等；participate需加in。空格后直接是in，因此选join。技巧：join in = take part in = participate in，注意搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Teenagers Create App to Help Save Food

Two students from Shenzhen, Li Hua and Wang Mei, have developed a mobile app called "Food Saver". The app aims to reduce food waste in school cafeterias.

The idea came to them last year when they saw large amounts of uneaten food being thrown away every day. "It was heartbreaking to see so much waste while many people in the world are still hungry," said Li Hua.

The app works in a simple way. Students can check the menu and order meals in advance through the app. The cafeteria staff then know exactly how much food to prepare. This prevents overproduction. Additionally, if students cannot eat at school unexpectedly, they can cancel their order two hours before lunchtime.

Since the app was introduced three months ago, food waste in the cafeteria has decreased by 40%. "The results exceeded our expectations," said Wang Mei. "We thought people might find the app troublesome at first, but most students actually find it convenient."

The app has won first prize in the city's Teenage Science Competition. Now, several other schools in Shenzhen have shown interest in using it. Dr. Zhang, a technology expert, commented, "This is a great example of how technology can solve real-life problems. These students have shown that innovation doesn't require complex technology—what matters is identifying a real need."

However, the team is not stopping here. They are working on new features, including a function that allows students to rate meals. This will help the cafeteria improve its service.
questions.stem: How does the "Food Saver" app help reduce food waste?
options: A. By providing free meals to students.
B. By allowing students to order meals in advance.
C. By closing the cafeteria early every day.
D. By limiting the amount of food per student.
questions.explanation: 根据第三段"Students can check the menu and order meals in advance through the app. The cafeteria staff then know exactly how much food to prepare. This prevents overproduction."可知，学生提前点餐让食堂知道准备多少食物，防止过量生产。A、C、D文中未提及。技巧：细节题定位关键词，在原文找同义句。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Teenagers Create App to Help Save Food

Two students from Shenzhen, Li Hua and Wang Mei, have developed a mobile app called "Food Saver". The app aims to reduce food waste in school cafeterias.

The idea came to them last year when they saw large amounts of uneaten food being thrown away every day. "It was heartbreaking to see so much waste while many people in the world are still hungry," said Li Hua.

The app works in a simple way. Students can check the menu and order meals in advance through the app. The cafeteria staff then know exactly how much food to prepare. This prevents overproduction. Additionally, if students cannot eat at school unexpectedly, they can cancel their order two hours before lunchtime.

Since the app was introduced three months ago, food waste in the cafeteria has decreased by 40%. "The results exceeded our expectations," said Wang Mei. "We thought people might find the app troublesome at first, but most students actually find it convenient."

The app has won first prize in the city's Teenage Science Competition. Now, several other schools in Shenzhen have shown interest in using it. Dr. Zhang, a technology expert, commented, "This is a great example of how technology can solve real-life problems. These students have shown that innovation doesn't require complex technology—what matters is identifying a real need."

However, the team is not stopping here. They are working on new features, including a function that allows students to rate meals. This will help the cafeteria improve its service.
questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Zhang's words in the last paragraph?
options: A. The app uses very complex technology.
B. Li Hua and Wang Mei are professional programmers.
C. Identifying real problems is important for innovation.
D. All schools in Shenzhen are using this app now.
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段Dr. Zhang的话"what matters is identifying a real need（重要的是发现真实需求）"，可推断出发现真实问题对创新很重要。A与原文"doesn't require complex technology"矛盾；B文中未提及他们是专业人士；D与"several other schools... shown interest"不符，只是感兴趣并非都在用。技巧：推理题基于原文但需合理推断，避免过度推理或与原文矛盾的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Teenagers Create App to Help Save Food

Two students from Shenzhen, Li Hua and Wang Mei, have developed a mobile app called "Food Saver". The app aims to reduce food waste in school cafeterias.

The idea came to them last year when they saw large amounts of uneaten food being thrown away every day. "It was heartbreaking to see so much waste while many people in the world are still hungry," said Li Hua.

The app works in a simple way. Students can check the menu and order meals in advance through the app. The cafeteria staff then know exactly how much food to prepare. This prevents overproduction. Additionally, if students cannot eat at school unexpectedly, they can cancel their order two hours before lunchtime.

Since the app was introduced three months ago, food waste in the cafeteria has decreased by 40%. "The results exceeded our expectations," said Wang Mei. "We thought people might find the app troublesome at first, but most students actually find it convenient."

The app has won first prize in the city's Teenage Science Competition. Now, several other schools in Shenzhen have shown interest in using it. Dr. Zhang, a technology expert, commented, "This is a great example of how technology can solve real-life problems. These students have shown that innovation doesn't require complex technology—what matters is identifying a real need."

However, the team is not stopping here. They are working on new features, including a function that allows students to rate meals. This will help the cafeteria improve its service.
questions.stem: What does the word "exceeded" in Paragraph 4 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，后面说"We thought...（我们原以为...）"，暗示实际情况比预期更好，浪费减少了40%，因此是"超出"预期。ex-表示"向外"，ceed表示"走"，走出去即"超过"。技巧：利用上下文逻辑（比预期好）和构词法（ex-向外）猜测词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Teenagers Create App to Help Save Food

Two students from Shenzhen, Li Hua and Wang Mei, have developed a mobile app called "Food Saver". The app aims to reduce food waste in school cafeterias.

The idea came to them last year when they saw large amounts of uneaten food being thrown away every day. "It was heartbreaking to see so much waste while many people in the world are still hungry," said Li Hua.

The app works in a simple way. Students can check the menu and order meals in advance through the app. The cafeteria staff then know exactly how much food to prepare. This prevents overproduction. Additionally, if students cannot eat at school unexpectedly, they can cancel their order two hours before lunchtime.

Since the app was introduced three months ago, food waste in the cafeteria has decreased by 40%. "The results exceeded our expectations," said Wang Mei. "We thought people might find the app troublesome at first, but most students actually find it convenient."

The app has won first prize in the city's Teenage Science Science Competition. Now, several other schools in Shenzhen have shown interest in using it. Dr. Zhang, a technology expert, commented, "This is a great example of how technology can solve real-life problems. These students have shown that innovation doesn't require complex technology—what matters is identifying a real need."

However, the team is not stopping here. They are working on new features, including a function that allows students to rate meals. This will help the cafeteria improve its service.
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. How to Use Mobile Apps Wisely
B. The History of Our School Cafeteria
C. Teenagers Create App to Reduce Food Waste
D. Dr. Zhang's Comments on Technology
questions.explanation: 文章主要介绍了两名中学生开发"Food Saver"应用程序减少食物浪费的事迹，包括应用的功能、效果和评价。A范围过大；B文中未提及食堂历史；D只是文末细节。C选项概括了主要人物（Teenagers）、动作（Create App）和目的（Reduce Food Waste）。技巧：主旨题要涵盖主要人物和事件，避免以偏概全或范围过大。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Hobbies

Many middle school students face a common problem: they want to do well in school, but they also want time for hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips that might help.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. The ten minutes before dinner or the bus ride home can be useful. (19)______ You could review vocabulary or read a few pages of a book. These short periods add up to a lot over a week.

(20)______ Don't give up your hobbies completely when exams are coming. Instead, reduce the time you spend on them. For example, if you usually play basketball for two hours, play for just one hour during exam weeks.

Remember, balance is key. Both study and hobbies are important for your growth.
options: A. However, finding the right balance is not always easy.
B. Then, put them in order of importance.
C. Finally, learn to adjust your hobby time.
D. For example, you can do some light studying during these moments.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 前文提出"想学好也想玩"的问题，后文"Here are some tips"给出建议，此处需要过渡句承上启下。A项"然而，找到正确的平衡并不总是容易的"承接上文的矛盾，引出下文的解决方法。B、C表示步骤，用于列举中；D是举例；E与后文"Don't give up your hobbies"矛盾。技巧：段首过渡句注意与上文话题衔接和引出下文。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Hobbies

Many middle school students face a common problem: they want to do well in school, but they also want time for hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips that might help.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. The ten minutes before dinner or the bus ride home can be useful. (19)______ You could review vocabulary or read a few pages of a book. These short periods add up to a lot over a week.

(20)______ Don't give up your hobbies completely when exams are coming. Instead, reduce the time you spend on them. For example, if you usually play basketball for two hours, play for just one hour during exam weeks.

Remember, balance is key. Both study and hobbies are important for your growth.
options: A. However, finding the right balance is not always easy.
B. Then, put them in order of importance.
C. Finally, learn to adjust your hobby time.
D. For example, you can do some light studying during these moments.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 前文说"写下所有作业和活动"，后文说"这样你就不会忘记重要任务"，中间缺少步骤。B项"然后，按重要性排序"承接上文写下任务，解释为什么不会忘记（因为排序了）。Then表示步骤顺序，与First呼应。技巧：注意逻辑顺序词First/Then/Finally。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Hobbies

Many middle school students face a common problem: they want to do well in school, but they also want time for hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips that might help.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. The ten minutes before dinner or the bus ride home can be useful. (19)______ You could review vocabulary or read a few pages of a book. These short periods add up to a lot over a week.

(20)______ Don't give up your hobbies completely when exams are coming. Instead, reduce the time you spend on them. For example, if you usually play basketball for two hours, play for just one hour during exam weeks.

Remember, balance is key. Both study and hobbies are important for your growth.
options: A. However, finding the right balance is not always easy.
B. Then, put them in order of importance.
C. Finally, learn to adjust your hobby time.
D. For example, you can do some light studying during these moments.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 前文说"空闲时间很有用"，后文举例"可以复习词汇或读几页书"，因此此处需要举例说明如何利用这些时间。D项"例如，你可以在这些时间做些轻松的学习"承上启下。For example是举例信号词，与后文You could...呼应。技巧：注意举例信号词for example/for instance。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
How to Balance Study and Hobbies

Many middle school students face a common problem: they want to do well in school, but they also want time for hobbies. (17)______ Here are some tips that might help.

First, make a schedule. Write down all your homework and activities. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks or waste time deciding what to do next.

Second, use small pockets of time. The ten minutes before dinner or the bus ride home can be useful. (19)______ You could review vocabulary or read a few pages of a book. These short periods add up to a lot over a week.

(20)______ Don't give up your hobbies completely when exams are coming. Instead, reduce the time you spend on them. For example, if you usually play basketball for two hours, play for just one hour during exam weeks.

Remember, balance is key. Both study and hobbies are important for your growth.
options: A. However, finding the right balance is not always easy.
B. Then, put them in order of importance.
C. Finally, learn to adjust your hobby time.
D. For example, you can do some light studying during these moments.
E. In addition, you should give up all your hobbies to focus on study.
questions.explanation: 前文有First... Second...，此处应该是最后一点建议。C项"Finally, learn to adjust your hobby time（最后，学会调整你的爱好时间）"符合顺序。后文"不要完全放弃爱好，而是减少时间"正是adjust（调整）的具体解释。技巧：注意顺序词First/Second/Finally/Finally的递进关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
